evidence_quotes.quote: mandatory end-of-life procedures for digital goods.
regulation which specifies acceptable end-of-life procedures offers a way for both parties benefit.
What matters is that consumers can have access to the product, even if the developer or publisher of the digital good is no longer interested in covering its running costs.

paragraphs.text: I wish to support this initiative for mandatory end-of-life procedures for digital goods. Publishers and developers cannot be expected to field the ongoing costs of server maintenance if the playerbase is too low. However, I strongly believe that the product itself should stay available for the consumer who purchased the project. To this end, regulation which specifies acceptable end-of-life procedures offers a way for both parties benefit. Whether it is through open-source servers or removal of always-online account services at a certain point, or some other method, that is up to the developers and publishers to decide. What matters is that consumers can have access to the product, even if the developer or publisher of the digital good is no longer interested in covering its running costs.
